# @zakkster/lite-headless/pin-input

PIN / OTP / one-time-code input. N input boxes with auto-advance,
backspace-handles-empty, paste-distribution, and ARIA wiring. The primitive
owns the value + which-box-is-active state; the consumer provides the
markup (N input elements) and styles. Built for 2FA flows, payment PINs,
and any short fixed-length code entry.

## Quick example

```js
import { createPinInput } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/pin-input";

const pin = createPinInput({
    length: 6,
    type: "numeric",
    onComplete: (code) => {
        verifyMfaCode(code).then(...);
    },
});

pin.attachRoot(rootEl);
for (let i = 0; i < 6; i++) {
    pin.attachInput(inputEls[i], i);
}
```

```html
<div class="pin-group">
    <input class="pin-box"> <input class="pin-box"> <input class="pin-box">
    <input class="pin-box"> <input class="pin-box"> <input class="pin-box">
</div>
```

The consumer styles `.pin-box` however they want — typically `width: 2.5rem; text-align: center; font-size: 1.5rem;` and an outline on `:focus`. Nothing about layout or sizing is owned by the primitive.

## Behavior

### Auto-advance

Typing a valid character in box N writes it and moves focus to box N+1. The last box stays focused (so the user sees what they typed) and `onComplete` fires.

### Backspace

- Box has a value -> clear it, stay focused.
- Box is empty -> move focus to previous box, clear that box too.
- Backspace at empty box 0 is a no-op.

This matches every native OTP UI and is what users expect; without it, "I typed a wrong digit" requires moving focus + backspacing separately.

### Paste

The killer feature for SMS OTP flows: the user gets `Your code is 123456` and copy-pastes the whole code. The primitive detects this:

- If the pasted (filtered) text length === `length`, fill all boxes from index 0 regardless of where the paste happened. This is the common case.
- Otherwise, insert at the current position, filling up to remaining capacity. Focus moves to the end of the inserted text.
- Pasted text is filtered by the pattern: pasting "1-2-3-4-5-6" into a numeric pin gives "123456".

### Pattern filtering

By default `type: "numeric"` (a `/[0-9]/` predicate). Other built-ins: `"alphanumeric"` = `/[A-Za-z0-9]/`. Pass a `RegExp` for custom patterns:

```js
createPinInput({ length: 4, type: /[0-9A-F]/ })   // hex
createPinInput({ length: 8, type: /[\u4e00-\u9fa5]/ })  // CJK
```

Invalid characters are silently dropped at the keystroke -- no flash of bad input, no rejection sound. The same filter applies to paste and `setValue`.

### Keyboard

- digit/char: write + auto-advance
- Backspace: clear current OR clear previous + back
- ArrowLeft / ArrowRight: navigate without writing
- Home / End: first / last box
- Enter: explicit submit (fires onComplete if complete)
- Any char failing the pattern is blocked at keydown (preventDefault) so it never visibly appears in the input

## Options

| Option            | Default        | Notes                                            |
| ----------------- | -------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `length`          | 6              | 1..16                                            |
| `type`            | `"numeric"`    | `"numeric"` / `"alphanumeric"` / `RegExp`        |
| `initialValue`    | `""`           | Filtered through `type` before being stored      |
| `onChange`        | -              | `(value, isComplete) => void`                    |
| `onComplete`      | -              | `(value) => void` — fires once per incomplete -> complete edge |
| `onInvalidPaste`  | -              | `(rawText) => void` — fires when paste has no valid chars |
| `inputAriaLabel`  | `(i, n) => "Digit N of N"` | Customize per-input aria-label    |
| `ariaLabel`       | `"One-time code"` | Applied to the root group                     |

## API

```js
pin.value()                          // string, 0..length chars
pin.isComplete()                     // boolean
pin.position()                       // 0..length-1, currently-active box

pin.setValue(s)                      // filters by pattern; sets focus to end
pin.setPosition(i)                   // move active pointer + focus
pin.clear()                          // reset to ""
pin.submit()                         // explicit; fires onComplete if complete
pin.focusInput(i)

pin.attachRoot(el)                   // -> off()
pin.attachInput(inputEl, index)      // -> off()
pin.destroy()
```

`attach*` returns an `off()` cleanup function that removes the wired attrs + listeners. Useful when the same primitive drives a re-rendered list of inputs.

## ARIA contract

Root:
- `role="group"`
- `aria-label="<ariaLabel>"` (default "One-time code")
- `data-pin-root`
- `data-pin-length="<N>"`
- `data-pin-state="incomplete|complete"`
- `data-pin-value-length="<K>"` (current filled count)

Each input:
- `data-pin-input`
- `data-pin-index="<i>"`
- `maxlength="1"`
- `aria-label="Digit <i+1> of <N>"` (customizable via `inputAriaLabel`)
- `inputmode="numeric"` when type is numeric
- `autocomplete="one-time-code"` on input 0 ONLY (iOS Safari uses this to surface the SMS auto-fill prompt; multi-box layouts conventionally put it on just the first input and the OS distributes the autofilled code via paste-like behavior, which the primitive's paste handler catches)

## Common patterns

### MFA verify flow

```js
const pin = createPinInput({
    length: 6,
    onComplete: async (code) => {
        statusEl.textContent = "Verifying…";
        try {
            await api.verifyMfa(code);
            location.href = "/dashboard";
        } catch (err) {
            statusEl.textContent = "Wrong code, try again";
            pin.clear();
        }
    },
});
```

### Numeric PIN with masking

The primitive doesn't mask values itself -- instead, apply CSS to the inputs to visually replace characters:

```css
.pin-box {
    /* Show bullets for typed chars but keep the real value in the DOM */
    -webkit-text-security: disc;
    text-security: disc;
}
```

Or use `type="password"` on the input element:

```html
<input class="pin-box" type="password">
```

The primitive's autocomplete + inputmode attrs are set on the `input` regardless of its DOM `type`, so screen readers + soft keyboards still get correct hints.

### Custom hex pattern

```js
const hexPin = createPinInput({
    length: 4,
    type: /[0-9A-Fa-f]/,
    ariaLabel: "Hex confirmation code",
});
```

### Paste-only flow

If the user shouldn't type but only paste (e.g. authenticator backup codes), use `aria-readonly` on inputs and disable the `input` listener via `attachInput`'s `off()` -- or, simpler, hide all but one input visually and let paste-fill distribute.

## Element wrapper

```html
<lite-pin-input length="6" type="numeric" aria-label="2FA code">
    <input data-pin-slot>
    <input data-pin-slot>
    <input data-pin-slot>
    <input data-pin-slot>
    <input data-pin-slot>
    <input data-pin-slot>
</lite-pin-input>
```

```js
const pin = document.querySelector("lite-pin-input");
pin.addEventListener("complete", (e) => {
    console.log("code:", e.detail.value);
});
pin.addEventListener("change", (e) => {
    progressBar.value = e.detail.value.length / 6;
});
```

### Reactive attributes

| Attribute    | Effect                                              |
| ------------ | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `length`     | 1..16 (default 6; read once on attach)              |
| `type`       | "numeric" or "alphanumeric" (default "numeric")     |
| `aria-label` | Root group label                                    |

### Imperative API + events

```js
host.value           // string (getter)
host.isComplete      // boolean (getter)
host.position        // 0..length-1 (getter)
host.setValue(s)
host.clear()
host.submit()
host.focusInput(i)

host.addEventListener("change",   (e) => /* e.detail = { value, isComplete } */);
host.addEventListener("complete", (e) => /* e.detail = { value } */);
```

The wrapper auto-attaches any descendant carrying `data-pin-slot` (in document order, up to `length` slots). MutationObserver picks up dynamic templates (shadow DOM hydration, framework re-renders).

## When NOT to use

- **A single text input with `maxlength`** -- if you don't need separate boxes, just use one input. The primitive's value is its segmented UX + paste-distribution.
- **Variable-length entry** -- this primitive enforces a fixed `length`. For variable-length codes, use a normal `<input>` with whatever pattern validation you need.
- **A masked PIN keypad with non-input buttons** -- this primitive expects real `<input>` elements. For numeric-keypad UIs (10 buttons + entered-state display), build a different component.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
